literal_description: A series of twelve black-and-white photographs. Each contains a centered arched doorway or window as dominant structural element. Arches vary in scale and elaboration — plain stone to ornate carving. Two images include partial figures standing within or before the arch. All images are framed perpendicularly to the arch plane. In 11 of 12 images, the area beyond the arch is lighter than the foreground. No captions on images. Archive folder label: 'threshold studies – undated'.
visual_relations: Camera perpendicular to arch plane in all 12 images
Light-dark gradient: brighter beyond arch in 11 of 12
Figures present in 2 of 12 — positioned at arch threshold, not beyond

symbolic_candidates.recurrence_evidence: 11 of 12 images share this structure; compositional choice repeated deliberately across varied architectural contexts
symbolic_candidates.confirming_evidence_needed: Photographer's statements, period context, archive curation history, full series scope

witness_context.archive_limitations: Archive label applied by unknown archivist. Origin of label (photographer intent vs. post-hoc curation) unknown. Full series scope unknown — these 12 images may be a curated subset. This is a constructed illustrative example.

interpretive_claims.claim: The consistent arch-centered, perpendicular framing is a deliberate compositional choice
interpretive_claims.basis: Repeated in 11 of 12 images across varied architectural types — too consistent to be incidental

interpretive_claims.claim: The arch-toward-light motif functions as a symbolic candidate for threshold or passage within this series
interpretive_claims.basis: Compositional intent (high confidence) + cross-traditional symbolic recurrence of arch-as-threshold form
interpretive_claims.scope: This series — not generalizable without external context

rejected_claims.claim: The series documents a specific building or location
rejected_claims.reason: Multiple distinct arch types and contexts; no location markers present

rejected_claims.claim: The photographer intended a meditation on death
rejected_claims.reason: Death is one possible reading within the threshold range but cannot be confirmed without external evidence. Reducing a candidate range to one specific meaning violates prohibited inference #4.

rejected_claims.claim: The archive label 'threshold studies' confirms the photographer's intent
rejected_claims.reason: Archive labels reflect labeler decisions, not necessarily subject intent. Treating metadata as ground truth violates prohibited inference #2.

rejected_claims.claim: The arch recurrence means this is religious photography
rejected_claims.reason: Secular arches appear; religious architecture is one context among several. Pattern does not establish category. Prohibited inference #1.

unresolved_questions: Whether archive label reflects photographer intent or archivist interpretation
Whether these 12 images are a complete series or curated subset
Geographic, temporal, and cultural context of photographer
Whether the 1 outlier image (no light-beyond-arch) is significant or incidental

safety_notes: Archive label noted but not treated as authoritative — see witness_context.archive_limitations
Symbolic candidacy of arch motif is medium confidence only; do not promote to confirmed symbol without external context
This is a constructed illustrative example, not an interpretation of a real archive
